chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Line item
triplets.object: Unit price at time of order

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A set of column-level attributes describing category hierarchy and product master data in a product catalog data model, including TB_CATEGORY fields (CATEGORY_ID, CATEGORY_NAME, PARENT_CATEGORY_ID) and TB_PRODUCT fields (PRODUCT_ID, PRODUCT_NAME).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |
| PRODUCT_NAME | VARCHAR(300) | Product display name | NO |
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO |
 | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
 | **Product Relationships**:
- A Product belongs to exactly one Category | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master
entities_post_resolution.definition: A central customer master dataset/table keyed by CUST_ID that serves as the source of truth for customer information referenced by other records (e.g., sales orders).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: An address record associated with a customer used for shipping and billing in an e-commerce domain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset. | Key Attributes: - Unique Customer ID - Valid email address (required for login) - One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Customer Relationships**:
- A Customer places zero or more Sales Orders | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | # Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER

entities_post_resolution.name: Email Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique email address used as the customer's login credential in the system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Key Attributes: - Unique Customer ID - Valid email address (required for login) - One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CUST_ID | INT | Unique customer identifier | NO |
| FULL_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Customer's full name | NO |
| EMAIL | VARCHAR(150) | Unique email address for login | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Line 1
entities_post_resolution.definition: Wireless Headphones are a consumer electronics audio device sold in discrete units at a fixed price, as shown in the provenance context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each | **Examples**:
- Line 2: 1 unit of USB Cable at €12.50

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Items
entities_post_resolution.definition: An order line item is a single product entry within a sales order, representing one product purchased in the order with its attributes (e.g., product reference, quantity, price).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Product Relationships**:
- A Product appears on zero or more Order Line Items | A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product | **Key Attributes**:
- Quantity of units ordered | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Business Rules**:
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates | **Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each | **Examples**:
- Line 2: 1 unit of USB Cable at €12.50 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a monetary transaction record linked to a Sales Order, identified by a unique PAYMENT_ID and containing the payment amount; a Sales Order can have one or more Payments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Payment amount | **Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships | **Payment Relationships**:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments | A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment Confirmed At
entities_post_resolution.definition: A timestamp indicating when payment for an order was confirmed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
| CUST_ID | INT | Customer who placed order | NO |
| ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO |
| TOTAL_AMT | DECIMAL(12,2) | Total order value | NO |
| STATUS_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | PENDING/CONFIRMED/SHIPPED/DELIVERED/CANCELLED | NO |
| PAYMENT_CONFIRMED_AT | DATETIME | Payment confirmation timestamp | YES |
 | **Business Rules**:
- Can only be created for confirmed orders with payment

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: An item in the catalog that has a unique identifier and belongs to exactly one category.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product | **Product Relationships**:
- A Product belongs to exactly one Category | **Product Relationships**:
- A Product appears on zero or more Order Line Items | **Order Line Item Relationships**:
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |
 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is the fulfillment unit representing a batch of goods sent in response to a sales order, identified by a unique shipment ID and tracking number, and a sales order can have one or more shipments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Tracking number | **Business Rules**:
- Can only be created for confirmed orders with payment | **Business Rules**:
- One order may have multiple partial shipments | A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Sales Order is the customer‑initiated purchase order, represented as a unique order header (ORDER_ID) linked to a customer and containing one or more line items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | **Sales Order Relationships**:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments | **Order Line Item Relationships**:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | **Payment Relationships**:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items | A Sales Order has one or more Payments | A Sales Order has one or more Shipments | An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | **Customer Relationships**:
- A Customer places zero or more Sales Or

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category
entities_post_resolution.definition: A database table that stores product records, typically including a foreign key referencing the category it belongs to.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | **Foreign Keys**:
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | Product          → TB_PRODUCT

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price At
entities_post_resolution.definition: Unit price at time of order is the price per unit recorded and used for a specific order, reflecting the historical price at the moment the order was placed.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |
| PRODUCT_NAME | VARCHAR(300) | Product display name | NO |
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO |
| UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price in EUR | NO |
 | | UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price at time of order | NO | | **Business Rules**:
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer records including identifying details, contact information, regional grouping, account creation timestamp, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products including identifiers, SKUs, names, category association, pricing, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores hierarchical category information for products, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores header-level information for customer sales orders, including identifiers, order timing, total amount, status, payment confirmation, delivery, and creation timestamps.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores line-item details for each order, including which product was ordered, the quantity, unit price, and the total line amount.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments processed against customer orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments, linking each shipment to its related order and warehouse, with shipment timing, tracking details, and current status.
